wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the AI-powered accessibility-first UI engine project overview. Explain the project's core purpose as a Next.js application that transforms natural language descriptions into production-ready React/Tailwind components with automated accessibility validation. Document the key value propositions including WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, multi-provider AI integration, and universal adapter system. Describe the target audience and primary use cases. Include high-level architecture overview showing the relationship between AI generation, validation, and preview systems. Provide technology stack summary covering Next.js 16, React 19, Prisma ORM, PostgreSQL, and various AI providers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples of common workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the system overview section focused on the AI-powered accessibility-first UI engine architecture. Explain the high-level system design, core value proposition, and overall technical approach. Document the multi-agent pipeline architecture, serverless deployment strategy, and key differentiators from basic AI wrappers. Include the system's focus on accessibility-first design, production-grade component generation, and multi-provider AI resilience. Provide conceptual overviews for beginners explaining the transformation from natural language to accessible React components, while including technical details for experienced developers about the deterministic pipeline approach and design system enforcement.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Generation Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the core generation pipeline that orchestrates the multi-stage AI component generation process. Document the complete workflow from intent processing through final code delivery, including the 12-step generation process: blueprint selection, model resolution, knowledge injection, prompt building, tool execution loops, code extraction, beautification, and validation. Explain the tiered pipeline configuration system that adapts generation parameters based on model capabilities and quality tiers. Detail the prompt engineering strategies including system/user message construction, context fitting algorithms, and token budget enforcement. Include implementation specifics of the generation loop, tool call protocols, and error handling mechanisms. Provide examples of successful generation workflows and troubleshooting common pipeline issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Registry & Catalog
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the component registry and catalog system. Document the component registry architecture that catalogs built-in components, their metadata, compatibility matrices, and usage patterns. Explain the component discovery mechanism, registration process, and lookup algorithms. Detail each component package including @ui/core (fundamental components), @ui/layout (layout primitives), @ui/forms (form controls), @ui/icons (iconography), @ui/utils (utility components), and @ui/tokens (design tokens). Document component metadata structure, dependency relationships, and version compatibility. Include guidelines for adding new components to the registry, component naming conventions, and prop interface standards. Provide examples of component usage patterns and integration with the generation pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: Accessibility Validation (WCAG 2.1 AA)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility validation system. Detail the rule-based validator implementation that performs static analysis on generated TSX code strings. Document all 12 accessibility rules including input labeling, button accessibility names, image alt text requirements, form labeling, heading hierarchy, keyboard accessibility, error announcements, color contrast requirements, and focus indicators. Explain the scoring system that calculates compliance scores based on severity levels (errors, warnings, info). Include examples of validation failures with specific WCAG criteria, suggested remediations, and the auto-repair functionality. Document the A11yReport schema and how violations are categorized and reported. Provide guidance on extending the rule set and customizing validation thresholds.

wiki_catalogs.name: Sandpack Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the CodeSandbox Sandpack integration that enables real-time component preview. Document the sandpack configuration system including dependency management, UI ecosystem integration, and environment setup. Explain how the SandpackPreview component renders generated code in a live preview environment. Detail the dependency injection mechanisms, UI component integration patterns, and theme support. Include configuration options for the sandpack environment, file system simulation, and runtime execution. Document the integration with the UI component libraries (@ui/* packages) and how generated components are rendered with proper styling and interactivity. Provide examples of preview workflows, configuration customization, and troubleshooting common preview issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Prisma ORM Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Prisma ORM configuration and database schema design. Detail the complete schema including entities for authentication (User, Account, Session), multi-tenancy (Workspace, WorkspaceMember, WorkspaceSettings), project management (Project, ProjectVersion), feedback system (GenerationFeedback), usage tracking (UsageLog), and vector embeddings (ComponentEmbedding, FeedbackEmbedding). Document the relationship mappings, foreign key constraints, unique indexes, and data types used throughout the schema. Explain the migration management strategy, including the purpose and impact of each migration file. Cover the database provider configuration for PostgreSQL, connection string handling, and environment variable management. Include examples of common queries, data access patterns, and performance optimization techniques. Address the integration with NextAuth.js for authentication and the workspace-based multi-tenancy architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Generation API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the generation endpoint (/api/generate). Document the POST method for generating React components with detailed request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and streaming capabilities. Include parameter specifications for intent objects, generation modes (component, app, depth_ui), model configuration, and optional overrides. Detail the multi-stage generation pipeline including validation, AI generation, expert review, accessibility repair, and safety validation. Provide examples of request payloads, response formats, error handling scenarios, and streaming response patterns. Document the refinement workflow for iterative improvements and the integration with workspace-specific AI adapters.

wiki_catalogs.name: Workspace Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the workspace management system. Document the multi-tenant architecture that isolates user data and workspaces, explaining how workspaces serve as containers for projects and users. Detail the workspace creation, configuration, and lifecycle management processes. Explain the workspace settings system including provider configuration, API key management, and workspace-specific preferences. Document the workspace member management system with role-based access control (OWNER, ADMIN, MEMBER). Include practical examples of workspace setup, member invitation workflows, and workspace configuration. Address workspace data isolation, security boundaries, and performance considerations for multi-tenant deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Vercel Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive Vercel deployment documentation for the AI-powered UI engine. Document the Vercel configuration in vercel.json including serverless function optimization, static asset handling, and performance settings. Explain Next.js serverless optimization through next.config.ts including experimental features, output tracing, and bundle optimization. Detail environment variable configuration for production, database connections, AI provider credentials, and security settings. Document the deployment pipeline including build processes, preview deployments, and production releases. Include performance optimization strategies such as ISR, static generation, and CDN configuration. Address scaling considerations, regional deployment options, and edge network utilization. Provide troubleshooting guides for common deployment issues, build failures, and runtime errors. Include monitoring setup, error tracking, and performance analytics integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication and authorization documentation for the AI-powered UI engine. Document the NextAuth.js integration with custom credentials provider, JWT session strategy, and bcrypt-based password verification. Explain the single-access password system using environment variable hashes, user roles and permissions, and session management with 7-day expiration. Detail the authentication flow from login page through session creation, including security considerations for credential handling and password hashing. Document the authorization callbacks, session token management, and integration with workspace-based access control. Include setup instructions for environment variables, security best practices for password storage, and troubleshooting common authentication issues. Address multi-tenant authorization patterns and user navigation components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Presentation Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the presentation layer architecture. Document the Next.js App Router implementation, React component hierarchy, and user interface design patterns. Explain how the frontend components interact with the backend API routes, the role of the IDE workspace interface, and the live preview system. Include details about component composition, state management, and user interaction patterns. Document the relationship between pages, components, and the overall user experience flow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Unit Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for unit testing implementation in the AI-powered UI engine. Document the Jest configuration, test environment setup, and coverage collection strategies. Explain component testing patterns for React components, integration testing for AI adapters and validation modules, and mocking strategies for external services like AI providers and database connections. Detail the test structure for security modules, accessibility validators, and schema validation. Include examples of testing AI provider adapters, validation functions, and utility modules. Document best practices for testing asynchronous operations, error handling, and edge cases. Address test isolation, dependency injection for testing, and maintaining test reliability across different environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Adapter Pattern
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Adapter Pattern implementation in the AI Generation Engine. Explain how the universal adapter interface abstracts different AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Ollama) behind a common contract. Document the adapter factory pattern that dynamically creates provider-specific adapters based on configuration and environment variables. Detail the security-conscious design that prevents client-side credential injection and ensures proper credential resolution through workspaceKeyService. Include concrete examples showing how new AI providers can be integrated, the adapter lifecycle, and how the caching wrapper enhances performance. Address the provider detection logic, fallback mechanisms, and error handling strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Adapter Architecture & Factory Pattern
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the AI adapter architecture and factory pattern implementation. Explain the base AIAdapter interface that defines the unified contract for all provider adapters, including generate() and stream() methods. Document the adapter factory pattern that resolves the correct adapter based on provider configuration, with detailed coverage of the getWorkspaceAdapter function and its credential resolution hierarchy. Explain the CachedAdapter decorator pattern that provides transparent caching for both synchronous generation and streaming responses. Detail the provider detection mechanism, model name resolution, and the hardening approach that prevents client-side credential injection. Include examples of the adapter lifecycle, error handling strategies, and the configuration hierarchy from workspace keys to environment variables.

wiki_catalogs.name: Intent Processing & Blueprint Selection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the intent processing and blueprint selection phase of the generation pipeline. Document how user intents are analyzed and transformed into actionable generation parameters. Explain the blueprint selection algorithm that matches user requirements to appropriate UI component patterns, including page type determination and design system compatibility. Detail the design rules application process that enforces consistency with established UI patterns. Cover the depth UI mode evaluation for complex interactive components. Include implementation specifics of the intent classification system, blueprint scoring mechanisms, and design rule conflict resolution. Provide examples of successful intent-to-blueprint mappings and troubleshooting common blueprint selection issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Generation Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the AI generation flow that transforms user intents into production-ready React components. Document the complete pipeline from intent processing through multi-stage generation with AI adapters, including the intelligent blueprint selection, design rule application, semantic knowledge base integration, and model-agnostic orchestration. Explain the step-by-step process: intent classification and preprocessing, model profile resolution, concurrent memory and knowledge retrieval, token budget management, prompt construction with intelligence context, agentic tool loop execution, code extraction strategies, and final beautification. Include detailed coverage of the model-agnostic architecture that enables seamless switching between different AI providers while maintaining consistent output quality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Model Registry & Profiles
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Model Registry system that defines all AI model capabilities and behaviors. Document the ModelCapabilityProfile interface including all properties: id, displayName, provider, tier classification, contextWindow, maxOutputTokens, idealTemperature, and capability flags (supportsSystemPrompt, supportsToolCalls, supportsJsonMode, streamingReliable). Explain the five tier classifications (tiny, small, medium, large, cloud) and their corresponding behavioral characteristics. Detail the model strength/weakness profiles, prompt strategy mappings, and extraction strategy selections. Include examples of registering new models, partial matching algorithms, and fallback mechanisms. Document the static registry architecture and how it integrates with the model resolution system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Generation API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the generation endpoint. Document the HTTP POST method, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements. Explain the generation pipeline integration, parameter validation, and error handling. Include examples of request payloads, response formats, and common use cases. Document the relationship with the AI generation engine and validation systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Agent Workflow Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the agent workflow management system that coordinates the multi-stage generation pipeline. Explain the sequential workflow from component generation through expert review to final repair. Document the generation stages including intent processing, blueprint application, design rule enforcement, and code extraction. Detail the expert reviewer agent's role in validating generated components against accessibility and security standards. Cover the repair pipeline coordination that applies automated fixes based on validation results. Include workflow state management, error handling mechanisms, and the decision logic for when to trigger repair actions. Provide examples of workflow execution paths and troubleshooting common workflow failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Provider Adapters
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI provider adapter system. Document the universal AIAdapter interface and how it enables provider-agnostic AI integration. Explain the adapter factory pattern implementation and dynamic adapter instantiation. Cover each supported provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, DeepSeek, Ollama) with their specific configurations, authentication methods, and capabilities. Include details about tool support, streaming capabilities, and response formatting. Document the fallback mechanisms, error handling strategies, and provider-specific optimizations. Provide practical examples of adapter usage and configuration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Generation Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the AI generation pipeline system. Document the multi-stage generation workflow including intent classification, component generation, expert review, and AI repair phases. Explain the tiered pipeline configuration, model selection strategies, and prompt engineering approaches. Detail the deterministic validation rules, code extraction mechanisms, and quality gates. Include implementation examples showing how user intents are processed through each stage and how the pipeline ensures consistent, high-quality output.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the AI-powered accessibility-first UI engine. Include detailed development environment setup instructions covering Node.js requirements, dependency installation using npm/yarn/pnpm/bun, and initial project configuration. Document environment variable setup for AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, DeepSeek, Ollama), database configuration with Prisma and PostgreSQL, and optional services like Upstash Redis. Provide step-by-step instructions for running the development server and accessing the application. Include first-generation example showing how to describe a UI component and see the generated output. Document common setup issues and troubleshooting steps. Make the content accessible to developers with varying experience levels while ensuring all technical prerequisites are clearly specified.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layered Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the layered architecture of the AI-powered UI engine. Describe each layer including the presentation layer (Next.js components and pages), API layer (serverless functions and routes), business logic layer (AI generation, validation, and orchestration), data access layer (Prisma ORM and database), and integration layer (AI providers and external services). Document the clear separation of concerns, data flow between layers, and how each layer contributes to the overall system functionality. Include component interactions, dependency relationships, and the deterministic nature of the generation pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Provider Adapters
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the universal AI adapter system that provides model-agnostic access to multiple AI providers. Explain the adapter factory pattern implementation, base adapter interface, and provider-specific adapter implementations for OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, DeepSeek, and Ollama. Document the adapter configuration system including authentication, model selection, and provider-specific parameters. Detail the unified interface that abstracts provider differences, enabling seamless switching between AI models. Include adapter registration, workspace-specific adapter resolution, and fallback mechanisms. Provide examples of implementing new adapters, configuring provider credentials, and handling provider-specific features like tool calls and streaming responses.

wiki_catalogs.name: Design System Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the design system integration and enforcement mechanisms. Document the blueprint engine that enforces design system rules during component generation and validation. Explain the design rules framework that defines accessibility standards, visual consistency requirements, and component composition guidelines. Detail the style DNA system that maintains design language consistency across generated components. Document the theming system integration, typography hierarchy enforcement, and color palette management. Include guidelines for extending design rules, customizing style DNA, and integrating new design system elements. Explain how generated components are validated against design system constraints and how violations are automatically resolved. Provide examples of design system enforcement in action and best practices for maintaining design consistency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Browser Safety Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the browser safety validation system that ensures generated code is secure and safe for execution in web browsers. Document the security scanning algorithms that detect potentially dangerous patterns, injection vulnerabilities, and unsafe code constructs. Explain the input validation and sanitization processes that clean user-generated content and prevent XSS attacks. Detail the browser compatibility checks and cross-browser safety measures. Include examples of detected security issues and their resolution strategies. Document the integration with the generation pipeline and how safety violations trigger quality gates. Provide guidance on configuring security policies and extending the validation rules for custom security requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing & Quality Assurance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the automated testing and quality assurance system. Document the Playwright test suite integration for end-to-end testing, including test scenarios, user workflow validation, and cross-browser compatibility testing. Explain the automated test generation system that creates comprehensive test suites for generated components. Detail the Jest configuration, test execution workflows, and coverage measurement strategies. Document the quality assurance processes including component testing, integration testing, and regression testing. Include examples of writing custom tests, running test suites, interpreting test results, and maintaining test coverage. Address debugging test failures, performance testing considerations, and continuous integration workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Vector Embeddings & Knowledge Base
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the vector embeddings system and semantic knowledge base implementation. Explain the pgvector integration with PostgreSQL for storing and querying high-dimensional embeddings. Document the embedding dimensions (768) and the text-embedding-004 model used for vector generation. Detail the ComponentEmbedding and FeedbackEmbedding models, including their purposes, fields, and relationships to the main application data. Explain the knowledge ingestion pipeline that processes UI components, templates, blueprints, and feedback data into searchable embeddings. Cover the semantic search implementation, similarity scoring algorithms, and filtered retrieval mechanisms using the 'source' field for domain-specific filtering. Document the embedding creation process, batch processing strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Include examples of vector search queries, similarity threshold tuning, and integration with the AI generation pipeline for retrieval-augmented generation workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Workspace & Project APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for workspace and project management endpoints. Document workspace CRUD operations including creation, configuration, member management, and settings updates. Cover project lifecycle APIs for creation, retrieval, updates, deletion, and version control. Detail the project refinement workflow, rollback capabilities, and collaborative features. Include authentication requirements, permission models, and workspace-scoped data isolation. Provide request/response examples for all endpoints, error handling patterns, and integration with the multi-tenant architecture. Document the relationship between workspaces, users, and project persistence.

wiki_catalogs.name: Project Persistence
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the project persistence system. Document the project lifecycle from creation to deletion, including project metadata, component types, and workspace associations. Explain the version control system for projects with detailed tracking of changes, version numbers, timestamps, and modification statistics. Detail the ProjectVersion model including code storage, intent metadata, accessibility reports, and thinking/review data. Document the project CRUD operations through API endpoints, including project listing, individual project retrieval, updates, and deletions. Explain the relationship between projects and workspaces, version history management, and collaborative editing workflows. Include examples of project creation, version comparison, and rollback scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Scripts
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for development and operational scripts in the AI-powered UI engine. Document the deployment setup script (deploy-setup.sh) including environment preparation, dependency installation, and initial configuration. Explain the ecosystem build script (build-ecosystem.mjs) covering component compilation, asset bundling, and distribution packaging. Detail database management scripts including seed-embeddings.ts for vector database initialization, seed-workspace-db.mjs for workspace data population, and ingest-ui.ts for UI component knowledge base updates. Document the knowledge synchronization script (sync-knowledge.ts) for maintaining semantic search capabilities. Include package.json scripts for common development tasks, testing automation, and maintenance operations. Provide step-by-step execution guides, parameter explanations, and error handling procedures. Address script customization for different environments, batch processing workflows, and integration with CI/CD pipelines. Include troubleshooting guides for script failures and performance optimization tips.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Protection & Privacy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed data protection and privacy documentation for the AI-powered UI engine. Document encryption implementations for secure data storage, API key management strategies, and privacy considerations for user-generated content. Explain data retention policies, user consent mechanisms, and compliance with privacy regulations. Detail the security scanning and sanitization processes for generated code, including input validation and output filtering. Document data lifecycle management, access control mechanisms, and audit logging. Include guidelines for handling sensitive workspace data, project information, and user preferences. Address data minimization principles, anonymization techniques, and secure deletion procedures. Provide privacy impact assessment considerations and data protection best practices for developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the API layer architecture. Document the Next.js serverless function implementation, routing patterns, and request/response handling. Explain the API endpoint structure, authentication mechanisms, and data flow between endpoints. Include details about error handling, validation, and the relationship between different API groups (generation, workspace, project, etc.). Document the serverless deployment characteristics and performance considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: End-to-End Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive end-to-end testing documentation using Playwright for the AI-powered UI engine. Document browser automation setup, cross-browser testing strategies, and user workflow validation. Explain testing the complete generation pipeline from intent submission to component preview, including accessibility validation and auto-repair workflows. Detail testing scenarios for workspace management, project persistence, and collaborative features. Include examples of testing real-time preview functionality, component rendering across different browsers, and responsive design validation. Document test data management, environment setup for e2e tests, and CI/CD integration. Address performance testing, accessibility compliance verification, and regression testing strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Pipeline Pattern
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Pipeline Pattern implementation that orchestrates the multi-stage generation workflow. Explain how the tiered pipeline architecture processes user intents through sequential stages: intent classification, component generation, expert review, and AI repair. Document the flow control mechanisms, stage validation, and error propagation strategies. Detail the streaming architecture that enables real-time feedback and progressive disclosure of generation results. Include examples of pipeline configuration, stage customization, and how the system maintains consistency across different generation tiers. Address performance optimization through parallel processing and caching strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Provider-Specific Implementations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for each AI provider implementation, covering the specific adapter classes for OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Ollama, and the UnconfiguredAdapter. Document the unique characteristics, authentication methods, and API differences for each provider. Explain how each adapter implements the base AIAdapter interface, including provider-specific parameters, response handling, and error management. Detail the OpenAI-compatible providers (Groq, LM Studio) and their special handling through the OpenAI adapter. Cover the UnconfiguredAdapter's role in graceful degradation and user-facing error messages. Include examples of provider-specific configuration, authentication flows, and any unique features like tool calls or streaming capabilities that each provider supports.

wiki_catalogs.name: Model Resolution & Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the model resolution and configuration phase of the generation pipeline. Document the model registry system that catalogs available AI models with their capabilities, token limits, and feature support. Explain the tiered pipeline configuration that adapts generation parameters based on model capabilities, including temperature settings, tool call rounds, and token budgets. Detail the default adapter resolution process that selects appropriate models based on workspace settings and availability. Cover the model profile system that tracks explicit registrations versus cloud fallback profiles. Include implementation specifics of model capability detection, pipeline parameter mapping, and fallback mechanisms. Provide examples of model selection scenarios and troubleshooting configuration conflicts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Validation Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the validation and quality assurance flow that ensures generated code meets accessibility standards and security requirements. Document the multi-layered validation process including WCAG 2.1 AA compliance checking, browser safety validation, syntax and semantic validation, and auto-repair capabilities. Explain the validation pipeline stages: accessibility rule enforcement with violation reporting, security scanning for XSS and other vulnerabilities, code quality checks for determinism and correctness, and intelligent repair mechanisms that can automatically fix common issues. Include coverage of the repair pipeline architecture that can use either rule-based fixes or AI-assisted repairs depending on model capabilities and complexity.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tiered Pipeline Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the tiered pipeline configuration system that automatically selects optimal generation parameters based on model capabilities. Document the five pipeline tiers (tiny, small, medium, large, cloud) and their corresponding strategies: fill-in-blank templates for tiny models, structured templates for small, guided-freeform for medium/large, and full freeform for cloud models. Explain the automatic parameter selection including temperature ranges, tool call configurations, blueprint token limits, and repair strategies. Detail the pipeline decision logic, fallback mechanisms, and performance optimizations. Include examples of configuring custom pipeline behaviors, optimizing for different use cases, and handling edge cases where model capabilities exceed expectations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Models API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the models endpoint. Document the HTTP GET method, available model listing, and model capability information. Explain the model registry integration, provider-specific details, and configuration options. Include examples of model responses and usage patterns for different AI providers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tool Execution System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the tool execution system that enables agents to perform external operations and retrieve contextual information. Explain the tool call protocol including message formatting, parameter serialization, and result processing. Document the available agent tools such as semantic search, code validation, knowledge retrieval, and example fetching. Detail the tool execution loop that handles multiple tool rounds with proper state management. Cover tool registration, function signatures, and error handling for failed tool executions. Include examples of tool usage patterns, tool call responses, and integration with the agent workflow. Address tool timeout handling, retry mechanisms, and performance optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Vector Embeddings System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the vector embeddings and semantic search system. Explain the embedding model integration, vector storage architecture, and semantic search implementation. Document the knowledge ingestion pipeline, embedding generation process, and similarity search algorithms. Cover the vector store configuration, indexing strategies, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) patterns. Include details about embedding dimensions, similarity thresholds, and performance optimization techniques. Provide examples of semantic search queries and integration with the generation pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Provider Adapter System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the universal AI adapter system. Document the adapter pattern implementation, base adapter interface, and provider-specific implementations. Explain the factory pattern for dynamic adapter instantiation, configuration management, and authentication handling. Detail the standardized request/response formats, error handling strategies, and fallback mechanisms. Include examples of adding new AI providers and extending the adapter system for custom integrations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture & Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the AI-powered accessibility-first UI engine. Describe the layered architecture including presentation layer (Next.js components), API layer (serverless functions), business logic layer (AI generation and validation), data access layer (Prisma ORM), and integration layer (AI providers). Document the core architectural patterns including Adapter Pattern for AI providers, Pipeline Pattern for multi-stage generation, Factory Pattern for dynamic adapter instantiation, and Observer Pattern for real-time feedback. Explain system boundaries, component interactions, and data flows from user intent through generation, validation, and preview. Include infrastructure requirements, scalability considerations, and deployment topology. Address cross-cutting concerns like security, monitoring, and multi-tenancy. Document technology stack integration and third-party dependencies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Design Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the core design patterns used throughout the AI-powered UI engine. Document the Adapter Pattern implementation for universal AI provider abstraction, the Pipeline Pattern for multi-stage generation workflow, the Factory Pattern for dynamic adapter instantiation, and the Observer Pattern for real-time feedback collection. Explain how each pattern addresses specific architectural challenges, provides flexibility for extensibility, and enables the system's resilience and scalability. Include concrete examples from the codebase showing pattern implementations and their benefits.

wiki_catalogs.name: Model Configuration & Selection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the model configuration and selection system that manages AI model capabilities, pricing, and performance characteristics. Document the model registry architecture including model profiles, capability flags, and tier classifications (tiny, small, large, cloud). Explain the tiered pipeline configuration system that automatically selects optimal generation parameters based on model capabilities. Detail the model resolution algorithm that chooses appropriate models based on intent complexity, workspace settings, and available resources. Include prompt style selection (structured, guided-freeform, freeform), token budget calculations, and fallback mechanisms. Provide examples of configuring custom models, optimizing for cost/performance, and handling model availability issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Code Quality & Syntax Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the code quality and syntax validation system that ensures generated components meet structural and syntactic requirements. Document the deterministic validation rules that check for proper React component structure, TypeScript compatibility, Tailwind CSS usage patterns, and component composition standards. Explain the syntax checking algorithms that validate JSX syntax, prop types, and component lifecycle requirements. Detail the semantic validation that ensures logical consistency in component relationships and data flow. Include examples of common validation failures and their fixes. Document the integration with the auto-repair system and how quality issues are automatically resolved. Provide guidance on customizing validation rules and adding new quality checks to maintain code standards.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Access Patterns & Performance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for data access patterns, query optimization, and performance strategies across the database layer. Detail the Prisma client initialization and connection pooling configuration. Document common query patterns used throughout the application, including workspace-scoped queries, project versioning, and feedback aggregation. Explain caching strategies for frequently accessed data like workspace settings and user permissions. Cover performance optimization techniques including query batching, lazy loading, and index utilization. Document the data access layer architecture separating business logic from database operations. Include examples of efficient query patterns for the generation history, project management, and workspace analytics. Address connection management, transaction handling, and error recovery strategies. Provide guidelines for monitoring query performance, identifying bottlenecks, and implementing database-level optimizations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Utility APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for utility endpoints including models, history, feedback, and manifest management. Document the models endpoint for AI provider configuration and availability checking. Cover history tracking APIs for generation records and user activity. Detail feedback collection endpoints for quality improvement and user insights. Include manifest generation for component metadata and dependency resolution. Provide request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and usage examples for each utility endpoint. Document integration patterns with the main generation pipeline and data persistence layers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the authentication and authorization system built on NextAuth.js. Document the user account management system including email/password authentication, OAuth provider integrations, and session handling. Explain the workspace-level permissions system with role-based access control (OWNER, ADMIN, MEMBER) and how permissions cascade through projects and workspaces. Detail the membership model that links users to workspaces with role assignments. Document the authentication flow including login, logout, password reset, and session management. Explain authorization patterns for API endpoints and frontend components. Include security considerations, session token management, and best practices for protecting workspace data. Provide examples of user registration, workspace invitation, and permission escalation scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Browser Safety Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive browser safety validation documentation for the AI-powered UI engine. Document the security scanning system that prevents XSS attacks, code injection, and other browser vulnerabilities in generated components. Explain the input validation and sanitization processes, DOMPurify integration, and content security policy enforcement. Detail the syntax validation, semantic analysis, and automatic repair capabilities for security issues. Document the integration with accessibility validation to ensure safe and compliant code generation. Include examples of detected security threats, remediation strategies, and validation rule configurations. Address cross-site scripting prevention, script tag filtering, and unsafe HTML attribute handling. Provide guidelines for extending validation rules and customizing security policies for different deployment environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Business Logic Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the business logic layer architecture. Document the core AI generation pipeline, multi-agent system, and validation workflows. Explain the adapter pattern implementation, universal AI provider interface, and quality assurance processes. Include details about the blueprint engine, design system enforcement, and auto-repair capabilities. Document the integration with external services and the deterministic nature of the generation process.

wiki_catalogs.name: Factory Pattern
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Factory Pattern implementation used throughout the system for dynamic object creation. Document the adapter factory that creates AI provider instances based on runtime configuration, the component registry factory for UI components, and the project manager factory for workspace-specific instances. Explain how factories encapsulate object creation logic, handle dependency injection, and enable runtime configuration changes. Include examples of factory method implementations, parameter validation, and how the factory pattern supports extensibility and testability. Address the relationship between factories and dependency injection containers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration & Authentication Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for AI provider configuration and authentication management systems. Explain the credential resolution hierarchy that prioritizes workspace-specific keys, environment variables, and graceful fallbacks. Document the workspaceKeyService integration for encrypted credential storage and retrieval. Detail the configuration error handling system and how ConfigurationError exceptions are properly surfaced to users. Cover the AI Engine Config panel functionality and how users configure provider credentials through the web interface. Explain the security measures that prevent client-side credential injection, including the server-only execution and credential validation processes. Include examples of proper configuration setup, troubleshooting common credential issues, and best practices for managing multiple provider keys in different workspaces.

wiki_catalogs.name: Prompt Engineering & Context Injection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the prompt engineering and context injection phase of the generation pipeline. Document the model-aware prompt building system that constructs optimal prompts for different AI providers and model types. Explain the context fitting algorithms that intelligently inject relevant knowledge while respecting token budgets, including progressive truncation strategies. Detail the semantic knowledge base integration that provides contextual information about UI patterns and best practices. Cover the memory system that retrieves relevant examples for similar component generations. Include implementation specifics of prompt template construction, context prioritization algorithms, and token budget enforcement mechanisms. Provide examples of effective prompt construction and troubleshooting context injection issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Persistence Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the data persistence flow that manages project lifecycle, version control, and historical tracking. Document the complete persistence pipeline including generation result storage, project metadata management, version control mechanisms, collaborative editing support, and historical revision tracking. Explain the data transformation from generation results to database entities, the version management system that tracks component evolution, and the collaborative features that enable team-based development workflows. Include coverage of the database schema design, relationship mapping between projects, versions, and workspaces, and the API endpoints that handle CRUD operations for persistent data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Prompt Strategy Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the prompt strategy management system that controls how AI models receive instructions during generation. Document the four prompt strategies: fill-in-blank (structured slot-filling), structured-template (numbered steps with blueprints), guided-freeform (style guidelines with design rules), and freeform (unrestricted generation). Explain the token budget allocation for each strategy, blueprint injection mechanisms, and context window optimization. Detail the prompt construction algorithms, template formatting, and strategy selection logic. Include examples of customizing prompt styles, optimizing for different model capabilities, and implementing hybrid approaches. Document the relationship between prompt strategies and model tier classifications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Workspace API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for workspace management endpoints. Document workspace CRUD operations, settings management, and user permission handling. Explain authentication requirements, role-based access control, and workspace isolation. Include examples of workspace creation, member management, and configuration updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Agentic Tool Loop Protocol
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the agentic tool loop protocol that manages iterative tool interactions between agents and external services. Explain the OpenAI-compatible tool call protocol including message formatting requirements, tool call validation, and response processing. Document the loop structure with configurable maximum rounds, tool call validation, and termination conditions. Detail the message sequence including system messages, user prompts, assistant tool calls, and tool responses. Cover error handling for malformed tool calls, invalid responses, and protocol violations. Include examples of successful tool loops, error scenarios, and debugging techniques for tool communication issues. Address performance considerations and optimization strategies for tool loop execution.

wiki_catalogs.name: Live Preview Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the live preview system integration with CodeSandbox Sandpack. Explain the sandbox configuration, dependency management, and real-time component rendering. Cover the security sandbox implementation, code isolation mechanisms, and performance considerations. Document the integration patterns between generated code and the preview environment. Include details about asset loading, CSS injection, and interactive element handling. Provide examples of preview customization and troubleshooting common integration issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Validation & Quality Assurance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the validation and quality assurance system. Document the multi-layer validation approach including WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility compliance, browser safety validation, and syntax checking. Explain the auto-repair capabilities, deterministic validation rules, and error correction mechanisms. Detail the integration between validation components and the generation pipeline. Include examples of validation failures and automatic repairs, along with configuration options for customizing validation rules.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Generation Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the AI generation engine that powers the UI component creation. Explain the multi-stage generation pipeline architecture including intent classification, expert reviewer stage, and AI repair agent. Document the universal adapter system that supports multiple AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, DeepSeek, Ollama) through a unified interface. Detail the model selection and configuration process, tiered pipeline configuration for different quality levels, and prompt engineering strategies. Include implementation details of the component generator, adapter factory pattern, and model registry. Document the relationship between user intents, generation parameters, and output processing. Provide examples of successful generation workflows and troubleshooting common generation issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow & Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the data flow and processing architecture of the AI-powered UI engine. Document the primary data pathways including the generation flow (User Intent → Intent Classifier → AI Adapter → Code Generator → Validation → Output), validation flow (Generated Code → Accessibility Checker → Security Validator → Auto-Repair → Final Output), persistence flow (Generation Results → Memory Store → Database → History API), and preview flow (Generated Code → Sandpack Config → Live Preview → User Feedback). Explain data transformations, validation points, caching mechanisms, and error handling throughout each flow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Multi-Agent Orchestration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the multi-agent generation orchestration system that coordinates intelligent agents for component generation and repair. Explain the agent workflow including the component generator, expert reviewer, and AI repair agent stages. Document the tool execution system that enables agents to perform external operations like code validation, semantic search, and knowledge retrieval. Detail the agentic tool loop protocol including tool call handling, result processing, and iterative refinement cycles. Include the repair pipeline orchestration, expert review mechanisms, and auto-repair coordination. Provide examples of agent interactions, tool usage patterns, and troubleshooting agent communication issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Auto-Repair System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the auto-repair system that automatically fixes common accessibility and security issues in generated code. Document the intelligent repair algorithms that analyze validation failures and apply targeted fixes to problematic code patterns. Explain the repair prioritization system that handles multiple simultaneous issues and resolves conflicts between different repair strategies. Detail the repair tracking mechanism that logs applied fixes and maintains audit trails. Include examples of common repair scenarios including accessibility violations, security issues, and code formatting problems. Document the repair confidence scoring and how the system determines the effectiveness of automated fixes. Provide guidance on extending the repair capabilities and customizing repair strategies for specific use cases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Access Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the data access layer architecture. Document the Prisma ORM implementation, database schema design, and migration management. Explain the encryption layer, data security measures, and workspace-aware data models. Include details about query patterns, caching strategies, and the relationship between encrypted data storage and application logic.

wiki_catalogs.name: Observer Pattern
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the Observer Pattern implementation for real-time feedback collection and metrics tracking. Explain how the metrics system observes generation events, validation results, and user interactions to provide real-time feedback. Detail the event-driven architecture that enables decoupled communication between components, the observer registration and notification mechanisms, and how the system maintains loose coupling while preserving functionality. Include examples of observer implementations, event propagation, and how the pattern supports extensibility for additional observers. Address performance considerations and memory management in long-running observation scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tool Execution Loops
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the agentic tool execution loops within the generation pipeline. Document the tool call protocol that enables AI agents to perform external operations, including the strict OpenAI-compatible message flow with assistant/tool message exchanges. Explain the tool execution system that handles parallel execution of multiple tool requests and proper message formatting. Detail the agent tools available for code generation assistance, including their capabilities and limitations. Cover the loop management that controls tool call rounds based on model capabilities and pipeline configuration. Include implementation specifics of tool call validation, error handling, and message protocol compliance. Provide examples of successful tool-assisted generations and troubleshooting tool execution failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Preview Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the live preview flow that enables real-time component rendering and interactive development. Document the complete preview pipeline from generated code to live browser preview, including Sandpack configuration, UI ecosystem integration, dependency management, and state synchronization. Explain the code transformation process that prepares generated components for the sandbox environment, the dependency resolution system that handles React, Tailwind, and UI library imports, and the real-time preview update mechanism that reflects changes instantly. Include coverage of the UI ecosystem integration that provides access to the internal component libraries, the security sandboxing that prevents malicious code execution, and the performance optimization techniques for smooth preview rendering.

wiki_catalogs.name: Model Resolution Algorithms
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the model resolution algorithms that automatically select appropriate models based on intent complexity, workspace constraints, and resource availability. Document the model matching logic including exact matches, partial matching (e.g., 'phi3:mini' matches 'phi3'), and fallback hierarchies. Explain the intent complexity scoring system, workspace resource limitations, and cost/performance optimization algorithms. Detail the resolution decision trees, priority ordering, and conflict resolution strategies. Include examples of customizing resolution rules, implementing priority queues, and handling model unavailability scenarios. Document the integration with workspace settings, usage analytics, and automatic scaling mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Project API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for project management endpoints. Document project CRUD operations, version control, and collaboration features. Explain project persistence, history tracking, and rollback functionality. Include examples of project creation, modification, and retrieval operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Communication Protocols
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the communication protocols used for agent-to-agent and agent-to-service interactions. Explain the standardized message formats, data serialization methods, and protocol versioning. Detail the adapter communication patterns that enable model-agnostic agent interactions. Cover the tool call protocol specifics including function signatures, parameter validation, and response schemas. Document error propagation mechanisms, timeout handling, and retry strategies. Include protocol negotiation for different model capabilities and fallback mechanisms. Provide examples of protocol messages, error responses, and debugging approaches for communication failures. Address security considerations and authentication requirements for agent communications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Blueprint Engine & Design System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the blueprint engine and design system enforcement capabilities. Explain the design rule enforcement, style DNA implementation, and component registry functionality. Detail how the system maintains design consistency, enforces brand guidelines, and manages component compatibility. Include examples of custom design rules, style customization options, and component extension mechanisms. Document the relationship between the blueprint engine and the generation pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the validation and quality assurance system that ensures generated components meet accessibility and safety standards. Document the WCAG 2.1 AA compliance checker implementation, browser safety validation for security scanning and sanitization, and syntax/semantic validation rules. Explain the auto-repair system that automatically fixes common accessibility and security issues. Detail the deterministic validation rules, compliance reporting mechanisms, and integration with the generation pipeline. Include examples of validation failures and their automatic repairs. Document the relationship between validation stages and quality gates in the generation pipeline. Provide guidance on customizing validation rules and extending the system with additional checks.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive technology stack documentation for the AI-powered UI engine. Document the core technologies including Next.js 16 with App Router for full-stack development, React 19 for component architecture, TypeScript for type safety, Prisma ORM for database abstraction, PostgreSQL (Neon) for data persistence, and Vercel AI SDK for AI integration. Include supporting technologies like Upstash Redis for caching, NextAuth.js for authentication, CodeSandbox Sandpack for live preview, and various AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, DeepSeek, Ollama). wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the integration layer architecture. Document the AI provider adapter system, external service integrations, and third-party API connections. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the code extraction and beautification phases of the generation pipeline. Explain the beautification pipeline that applies deterministic formatting rules to ensure consistent code structure regardless of model output variations. Detail the completeness validation that checks for essential code elements like exports and closing braces. Cover the extraction confidence scoring system that helps identify potentially problematic outputs. Include implementation specifics of extraction algorithms, beautification transformation rules, and validation criteria. Cover workspace management APIs, project CRUD operations, history tracking, and feedback collection endpoints. Detail the models API for AI provider configuration and availability. Include authentication methods, error handling strategies, rate limiting, and security considerations. Include client implementation guidelines, SDK usage examples, and integration patterns. Address API performance optimization, monitoring, and troubleshooting.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security & Privacy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive security and privacy documentation for the AI-powered UI engine. Address code formatting standards, linting rules, and continuous integration requirements.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# AI-Powered Accessibility-First UI Engine: Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
The AI-Powered Accessibility-First UI Engine is a sophisticated Next.js full-stack application designed to transform natural language UI descriptions into production-ready, highly accessible React/Tailwind components. Unlike basic AI wrappers, this system employs a multi-agent pipeline with strict design system blueprints, automated accessibility validation, and resilient adapter patterns supporting multiple AI providers.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Accessibility-First Approach**: Ensures WCAG 2.1 AA compliance through automated validation and repair
- **Multi-Agent Pipeline**: Implements Generator → Reviewer → Repair Agent workflow for quality assurance
- **Universal Compatibility**: Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, DeepSeek, and local Ollama models
- **Production-Ready Output**: Generates TypeScript/TSX components with automated testing and validation
- **Serverless Scalability**: Built for Vercel deployment with PostgreSQL and Redis caching

### Target Audience
- Frontend developers seeking AI-assisted component generation
- Teams requiring accessible UI component creation
- Organizations needing standardized design system enforcement
- Developers working with multiple AI providers and models

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
The system follows a modular architecture with clear separation of concerns:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Client Interface] --> B[Next.js App Router]
    B --> C[API Routes]
    C --> D[AI Generation Pipeline]
    D --> E[Adapter Factory]
    E --> F[Multiple AI Providers]
    D --> G[Validation Layer]
    G --> H[Accessibility Checker]
    G --> I[Security Validator]
    D --> J[Code Generation]
    J --> K[Component Renderer]
    K --> L[Sandpack Preview]
```

### Design Patterns
- **Adapter Pattern**: Universal interface for multiple AI providers
- **Pipeline Pattern**: Multi-stage processing with quality gates
- **Singleton Pattern**: Prisma client management for serverless efficiency
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic adapter instantiation based on configuration
- **Observer Pattern**: Real-time code change tracking and feedback collection

### System Relationships
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    Client[Web Client] --> API[API Layer]
    API --> Auth[Authentication]
    API --> Gen[Generation Engine]
    Gen --> Adapter[Adapter Factory]
    Adapter --> Providers[AI Providers]
    Gen --> Validation[Validation Layer]
    Validation --> A11y[Accessibility Validator]
    Validation --> Security[Security Scanner]
    Gen --> Storage[Database Layer]
    Storage --> Prisma[Prisma ORM]
    Storage --> Redis[Redis Cache]
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Application Root**: `app/layout.tsx` - Global layout with authentication providers
- **Home Page**: `app/page.tsx` - Main interface coordinating the generation pipeline
- **API Endpoints**: `app/api/*` - Serverless routes handling generation requests

### Core Modules
- **AI Generation**: `lib/ai/componentGenerator.ts` - Orchestrates the multi-stage generation pipeline
- **Adapter System**: `lib/ai/adapters/index.ts` - Universal interface for AI provider integration
- **Validation**: `lib/validation/a11yValidator.ts` - WCAG 2.1 AA compliance checking
- **Security**: `lib/validation/security.ts` - Browser safety validation and input sanitization

### Configuration Approach
- **Environment Variables**: `package.json` - Dependencies including Next.js 16, Prisma, and AI SDKs
- **Next.js Configuration**: `next.config.ts` - Serverless optimization and security headers
- **Database Schema**: `prisma/schema.prisma` - PostgreSQL schema with vector embeddings

### External Dependencies
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Next.js 16] --> B[React 19]
    C[Prisma] --> D[PostgreSQL]
    E[Upstash Redis] --> F[Caching Layer]
    G[Vercel AI SDK] --> H[Streaming Support]
    I[Playwright] --> J[Testing Framework]
    K[CodeSandbox Sandpack] --> L[Live Preview]
```

### Integration Points
- **AI Providers**: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, DeepSeek, Ollama, Groq
- **Database**: Neon PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM
- **Caching**: Upstash Redis for response caching
- **Authentication**: NextAuth.js with JWT strategy
- **Validation**: Custom accessibility and security validators

### Component Relationship Diagram
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[GeneratedCode Component] --> B[CodeMirror Editor]
    C[SandpackPreview] --> D[CodeSandbox Integration]
    E[PipelineStatus] --> F[Generation Progress]
    G[FeedbackBar] --> H[User Feedback Collection]
    I[AIEngineConfigPanel] --> J[Provider Configuration]
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
The system provides a comprehensive UI generation workflow with multiple quality assurance layers:

1. **Intent Classification**: Automatic detection of component, app, or depth UI generation
2. **Multi-Agent Generation**: Three-stage pipeline with expert review and repair
3. **Accessibility Validation**: Automated WCAG 2.1 AA compliance checking
4. **Security Validation**: Browser-safe code generation with input sanitization
5. **Live Preview**: Interactive Sandpack-based component preview
6. **Test Generation**: Automated RTL and Playwright test creation

### Implementation Highlights
- **Generation Pipeline**: `app/api/generate/route.ts` - Complete 10-step generation workflow
- **Intent Processing**: `lib/intelligence/inputValidator.ts` - Structured intent parsing
- **Blueprint System**: `lib/intelligence/blueprintEngine.ts` - Design system enforcement
- **Vector Store**: `lib/ai/vectorStore.ts` - Semantic knowledge base integration

### Feature Architecture Diagram
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle
    Idle --> Parsing: User Input
    Parsing --> Generating: Intent Classified
    Generating --> Validating: Code Generated
    Validating --> Testing: Accessibility Passed
    Testing --> Complete: Tests Generated
    Validating --> Repairing: Accessibility Issues Found
    Repairing --> Validating: Repaired Code
    Generating --> Error: Generation Failed
    Validating --> Error: Validation Failed
    Error --> Idle: Reset
    Complete --> Idle: New Request
```

### Quality Assurance Pipeline
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant API as API Route
    participant GEN as Generator
    participant REV as Reviewer
    participant VAL as Validator
    participant SEC as Security
    
    U->>API: Generation Request
    API->>GEN: Generate Component
    GEN-->>API: Raw Code
    API->>REV: Expert Review
    REV-->>API: Review Results
    API->>VAL: Accessibility Check
    VAL-->>API: A11y Report
    API->>SEC: Security Validation
    SEC-->>API: Safety Check
    API-->>U: Final Component
```

## 5. Conclusion and Recommendations

### Project Architecture Summary
The AI-Powered Accessibility-First UI Engine demonstrates a mature, production-ready architecture that successfully balances AI-driven generation with rigorous quality assurance. The system's multi-agent pipeline, universal adapter pattern, and comprehensive validation layers create a robust foundation for automated UI component generation.

### Architectural Strengths
- **Modular Design**: Clear separation between generation, validation, and presentation layers
- **Provider Agnostic**: Universal adapter pattern supports multiple AI providers seamlessly
- **Quality First**: Multi-layer validation ensures high-quality, accessible output
- **Scalable Infrastructure**: Serverless-first design optimized for Vercel deployment
- **Comprehensive Testing**: Unit tests covering critical components like adapters and encryption

### Potential Improvement Areas
- **Multi-Tenancy**: Current workspace system needs NextAuth/Clerk integration
- **CI/CD Pipeline**: Missing SonarQube integration and comprehensive test coverage
- **Documentation**: Enhanced API documentation and developer guides
- **Performance Monitoring**: Additional observability metrics beyond current telemetry

### Actionable Recommendations
1. **Implement Multi-Tenant Authentication**: Integrate NextAuth.js for proper session-based multi-tenancy
2. **Enhance Testing Coverage**: Expand Jest test suite to achieve 80%+ coverage
3. **Add Docker Support**: Create docker-compose.yml for on-premises deployment
4. **Improve Documentation**: Create comprehensive API documentation and integration guides
5. **Performance Optimization**: Implement additional caching strategies and monitoring

### Next Steps for Project Evolution
- **Vercel AI SDK Migration**: Replace manual streaming with official SDK for better frontend integration
- **Quality Gates**: Implement SonarQube integration and continuous quality monitoring
- **Enterprise Features**: Add project collaboration, version control, and audit trails
- **Advanced AI Integration**: Support for multimodal inputs and advanced reasoning capabilities

Sources:
- [package.json](package.json)
- [README.md](README.md)
- [ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)
- [layout.tsx](app/layout.tsx)
- [auth.ts](lib/auth.ts)
- [prisma.ts](lib/prisma.ts)
- [generate/route.ts](app/api/generate/route.ts)
- [componentGenerator.ts](lib/ai/componentGenerator.ts)
- [adapters/index.ts](lib/ai/adapters/index.ts)
- [a11yValidator.ts](lib/validation/a11yValidator.ts)
- [GeneratedCode.tsx](components/GeneratedCode.tsx)
- [SandpackPreview.tsx](components/SandpackPreview.tsx)
- [page.tsx](app/page.tsx)
- [next.config.ts](next.config.ts)

</blog>
